hard_rules.message: Frame masks as periodic extra care, not a baseline replacement for conditioner.
Do not present normal masks as scalp treatments, permanent split-end repair, structural rebuilding, or future-damage prevention.
Use select_products or supported product data for mask weight, balance, intensity, fit, category membership, and requested-property caveats; do not infer these from names or marketing.

Route where/how/often/order questions as routine_explanation with routine_intent none unless the user asks to add, remove, replace, or change a mask in a saved or current routine, which requires build_or_fix_routine.
Keep general mask cadence and order flexible; exact timing, cadence, follow-up conditioner, scalp use, or product protocol requires product metadata.

Do not claim structural repair, repair from inside, or bond-repair effects from a normal mask; compare with the bondbuilder lane when the user asks about snapping, gummy/elastic wet hair, bleach damage, or bond repair.

soft_rubrics.message: Explain when a mask helps and when conditioner is enough.

When protein/moisture balance, chemical stress, frizz, roughness, coated feel, brittleness, or limp hair is user-raised or materially signaled, connect the mask role to that profile signal without turning it into a hard diagnosis.
Keep usage practical and flexible: usually after shampoo, lengths and ends, rinse well, gelegentlich/alle paar Waeschen/bei Bedarf, and stretch use if hair feels heavy, too soft, or flat.
For dry/frizzy lengths inside a lightweight-routine decision, prefer a light occasional mask as the main add-on; oil may be only a tiny tips/gloss finish.
Separate type/kind education with no product cards from which-mask/options/count/recommendation requests that require select_products and specific_products.

For named masks and claim checks such as protein-rich/free, moisture-focused, lightweight/rich, color-protective, repair-supported, scalp-directed, silicone-free, coconut-free, oil-free, or profile fit, use product_detail with product metadata or select_products.
For safety symptoms, use care_category none; if the user links symptoms to a mask, mention the suspected trigger only in safety wording.

required_grounding.when: Concrete mask names, category membership, product order, weight, balance, intensity, fit, named-product detail, claim checks, or unsupported requested-signal caveats are mentioned.

Exact mask timing, cadence, order, follow-up conditioner, scalp use, color-protection, repair support, or profile-specific product claims are mentioned; use product metadata or select_products rather than category prose.
The user asks to add, remove, replace, or change a mask in a saved or current routine.

ask_when.condition: Damage level, weight sensitivity, current conditioner fit, or whether the issue is dryness, damage, buildup, or scalp symptoms would materially change whether a mask is useful.
ask_when.question_policy: Ask at most one targeted follow-up; otherwise give a conservative optional-mask answer.

ask_when.condition: Protein/moisture direction would materially change the answer and the user raised it, the profile signals it, or rough, limp, brittle, coated, or recently chemically stressed hair makes it useful.
ask_when.question_policy: Ask at most one targeted follow-up; do not ask protein/moisture by default.
